user_query:
Please set up a small event-triggered automation that watches the workspace input/ directory for new incident files named incidents_*.jsonl and, when a new file appears, processes it using the provided parser, composes a quarterly summary report, and drafts an email update. For verification, run it once to process the included input/incidents_2024Q4.jsonl. Required behavior and deliverables:

1) Detection and processing: Implement a script or small service that detects the presence of input/incidents_*.jsonl. For this run, it should detect input/incidents_2024Q4.jsonl and execute tools/incident_parser.py on that file. Capture the parser's stdout to a CSV and stderr to a log file.

2) Outputs produced after processing input/incidents_2024Q4.jsonl:
- out/data/parsed_incidents_2024Q4.csv (CSV from parser stdout)
- out/logs/2024Q4_parser.log (full stderr from parser)
- out/summary_2024Q4.md (a human-readable quarterly summary with at least these sections: Overview; Counts by severity; Incident list with date, plant, type, severity, status, summary; Parser diagnostics showing parsed_records, total_lines, errors, warnings, and any error lines)
- out/email_draft_2024Q4.txt (a plain-text email draft that includes a To line, BCC line, Subject line, and a short body summarizing key incidents and the counts by severity)
- out/state/processed_files.json (a simple state file listing processed input filenames so subsequent runs don’t reprocess already handled files)

3) Constraints and cross-checks:
- Use tools/incident_parser.py exactly as provided to parse the JSONL. Do not reimplement parsing; rely on the command’s stdout (CSV) and stderr (diagnostics/errors).
- Read local files and extract relevant content from both input/incidents_2024Q4.jsonl and input/contacts.yaml. The email draft must address recipients from contacts.yaml: To should include all emails under research_group and departmental_list; BCC should include all emails under students.
- In out/summary_2024Q4.md, include a “Parser diagnostics” section that parses out/logs/2024Q4_parser.log to report parsed_records, total_lines, errors, and warnings. Also include the exact error lines if any. Cross-validate by counting rows in out/data/parsed_incidents_2024Q4.csv and stating this count alongside parsed_records; if they differ, note the discrepancy in the diagnostics section.
- The email draft (out/email_draft_2024Q4.txt) must reflect the diagnostics: if errors > 0, prepend “[Diagnostics: errors present]” to the Subject line. The body should summarize the incidents (date, plant, type, severity, status) and provide counts by severity.
- If the parser emits any error or warning messages, they must be preserved in out/logs/2024Q4_parser.log and summarized in the report.

This should run locally without any external services. After implementing, execute the automation once so the specified output files are created in the out/ paths above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
